meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Antigravity Project Template to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with no portfolio. Use our Antigravity project template and Claude Code to build revenue-generating SaaS apps at lightning speed.
content.answer_block: Overcome the 'no portfolio' trap by deploying high-utility SaaS micro-tools using the Antigravity project template. By leveraging Claude Code for rapid architecture and implementation, you create tangible, revenue-generating assets that demonstrate technical mastery and business acumen to potential clients, investors, and users without needing years of legacy work.
content.intro:
The cold reality of the SaaS market is that nobody cares about your potential; they care about your proof. You are sitting on a brilliant idea, but the 'no portfolio' barrier acts as an invisible wall between you and your first $10k in ARR. Potential users don't want to hear about what you could build—they want to see what you have already shipped.

We are breaking that cycle today. By leveraging the Antigravity project template, we turn the tide from 'aspirational developer' to 'proven founder.' This isn't about padding a resume; it is about building high-velocity, production-ready software that solves real problems while simultaneously validating your technical authority.

steps.title: Define the High-Utility Micro-SaaS
steps.description: Select a niche problem that can be solved in a weekend. Focus on functionality over aesthetics to demonstrate rapid delivery.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a project structure for a micro-SaaS that solves [Insert Niche Problem] with a focus on authentication, database schema, and payment gateway integration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'initialize project scaffolding with Antigravity template for a [Niche] tool'

steps.title: Configure the Antigravity Base
steps.description: Deploy the core architecture. This ensures your project is scalable from day one, even as a prototype.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the project environment for a serverless deployment, ensuring API routes are protected and database connections are optimized for low latency.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure environment variables and database orm for production readiness'

steps.title: Implement Core Business Logic
steps.description: Focus strictly on the 'Value-Add' feature that makes users pull out their credit cards.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the core service logic to process [User Input] into [Expected Output] with error handling and logging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement core business logic in /services directory with unit tests'

steps.title: Integrate Stripe for Monetization
steps.description: A portfolio piece without a payment flow is just a hobby. Add Stripe to prove your product has business intent.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe checkout integration that handles successful payments and updates the user subscription status in the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add stripe checkout webhook and subscription management logic'

steps.title: Ship to Production
steps.description: Deploy your project to a live domain. A live link is worth 100 screenshots.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the deployment configuration and identify any bottlenecks before pushing to production.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'audit production config and deploy to production environment'

pro_tips: Focus on 'Hard Problems': Don't build a To-Do list; build a tool that automates a workflow that currently takes a user 2 hours.
Prioritize Data Integrity: Even in a prototype, treat your data models like they are holding production traffic to avoid technical debt.
Ship the 'Boring' Stuff: A polished authentication flow and a clean 404 page signal more professionalism than a flashy animation.
Use Claude Code for Documentation: Use the CLI to generate a README that explains the 'Why' behind your architectural choices, not just the 'How'.
Analytics First: Integrate a lightweight tracker like PostHog immediately; showing you know how to track user behavior is a massive competitive advantage.
content.next_steps: Once your project is live, treat your own product like a customer. Market it on IndieHackers, Twitter, and Reddit. Use the feedback loop to iterate the next feature set, and keep building until your portfolio is a list of revenue-generating assets.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a coder; you are a builder with a live, monetized product. Stop waiting for permission and start building your legacy. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
